title: Microsoft Teams node unable to connect due to missing permission
symptom: Users are unable to connect their Microsoft Teams account in n8n due to a missing permission that is incorrectly documented.
not_this_if: Other Microsoft nodes work without issues
Permissions are correctly set and consented
root_cause: The permission `Subscription.ReadWrite.All` mentioned in n8n documentation does not exist in Microsoft Graph, leading to confusion and connection failures for users trying to authenticate their Microsoft Teams and OneDrive accounts.
fix_steps: Skip the `Subscription.ReadWrite.All` permission in your app registration.
Ensure admin consent is granted for the permissions `ChannelMessage.Read.All`, `Chat.Read.All`, and `Team.ReadBasic.All`.
Check that your Azure app registration is set to 'Accounts in any organizational directory'.
Re-create your credentials in n8n after confirming the permissions are properly consented.
agent_instruction: Follow the steps to adjust permissions and re-create credentials for Microsoft Teams and OneDrive nodes.
